name: Carbone
tips: Be logged into Resy at 9:59 AM
Refresh at exactly 10:00:00 - seconds matter
No walk-ins accepted, ever
Set Resy notifications for cancellations

name: Don Angie

walkIn.advice: Walk-in list opens at 4:30 PM sharp; arrive early, put your name down, then explore the neighborhood. Bar seats are available for walk-ins.

tips: SWITCHED TO OPENTABLE MAY 2025 - no longer on Resy
Reservations release 7 days in advance at 9 AM
Bar seats available for walk-ins
The Lasagna For Two is worth the effort

name: Torrisi

walkIn.advice: 12-seat bar for walk-ins (parties of 1-2 only); show up at 4:15 PM for the 5 PM seating. Weeknight waits can be 4+ hours.

tips: Same release as Carbone (Major Food Group)
Lunch reservations are easier to get
Bar is first-come, first-served

name: 4 Charles Prime Rib

walkIn.advice: Bar area is available for walk-ins.

tips: $5/person reservation fee charged at booking
Very intimate brownstone setting
No high chairs or strollers
Reservations sell/trade on secondary market (against policy)
signatureDish: Prime Rib (English, Charles, or King Cut)

name: The Polo Bar
tips: Phone reservations ONLY - no online booking for prime times
Expect 30-60 minute hold times
Amex Platinum concierge can help
Try calling at 4 PM day-of for cancellations
Pre/post theater times available on OpenTable

name: Atomix
releaseDay: 1st of each month (entire month releases at once)
tips: Only 14 seats at the counter
$395-420/person prepaid at booking
Two Michelin stars, #8 World's 50 Best
Set calendar reminder for 1st of month at 2:55 PM

name: Via Carota

walkIn.advice: Most tables are walk-in; arrive early (before 5 PM). Wait at Bar Pizzalino next door (same owners) and they'll text you.

tips: Walk-ins are the primary way to dine here
Sunday at 5 PM is a good time to try
Taylor Swift's favorite spot
The cacio e pepe is legendary

name: Lilia

walkIn.advice: Bar, patio, and piazza seats are held for walk-ins; arrive right at 4 PM when they open and put your name down for a later slot. Walk-in waits run 30 min to 3 hours.

tips: Call between 10 AM - 4 PM to speak to reservationist
Weekend reservations book within hours
Check Resy around 2 PM day-of for cancellations
Walk-in waits: 30 min to 3 hours

name: Rao's
tips: Tables are 'owned' by regulars and passed down through generations
Only 10 tables total (4 available to non-regulars)
Try the bar - it's your only realistic shot
Cash only if you somehow get in
Call the phone number listed on website for best chance

name: The Corner Store

walkIn.advice: 13-seat bar is first-come, first-served; arrive before opening for the best chance. Wait times run 1-4 hours.

tips: Taylor Swift effect - exploded in popularity late 2024
Smart elegant dress code required
No parties larger than 4
Reservations disappear within seconds of release
Be ready at 9:55 AM on Resy

name: Tatiana

walkIn.advice: 6 bar seats plus 32 patio seats are available for walk-ins; arrive by 4:30 PM as the line forms early.

tips: #1 Restaurant in NYC (NYT 2024)
Prime 5-7 PM slots gone in minutes
700-2000 people on notify list daily
Try the 7:30-8 PM gap between seatings
Refresh Resy throughout day for cancellations

name: Cote Korean Steakhouse

walkIn.advice: Standing room only at the bar; Undercote (the downstairs bar) is walk-in only.

tips: Better luck with parties of 4-6 (table setup)
Two-tops only available at first/last seating
Michelin starred
Phone reservationists available 11 AM - 10 PM daily

name: Le Bernardin

walkIn.advice: Bar and lounge accept walk-ins for drinks and lighter fare.

tips: Three Michelin stars
NOT on OpenTable - use Resy or call directly
Weekend dinner slots fill within hours
Lunch reservations much easier to get
Jacket required for men

name: Le Veau d'Or

walkIn.advice: 5-seat bar accepts walk-ins for drinks, lunch, or dinner.

tips: Reopened July 2024 by Frenchette team
Only seats up to 4 per table in main dining room
Prix fixe $135/person dinner, $85 lunch
$50/seat fee if party size decreases after booking
James Beard Outstanding Restaurateur 2025

name: The Four Horsemen

walkIn.advice: Arrive at the 5:30 PM opening for walk-in bar seats.

tips: Co-owned by LCD Soundsystem's James Murphy
Exceptional natural wine program
Small plates meant for sharing
Early morning release time - set alarm for 6:55 AM

name: Ha's Snack Bar

walkIn.advice: Primarily walk-in; the line starts forming at 4:45 PM for the 5:30 PM opening. A Thursday at 9:30 PM was quoted a 4-hour wait.

tips: Midnight release time is unusual - set alarm
Very limited Resy availability - walk-in is often easier
Sister restaurant Bistrot Ha opened Dec 2025 at 137 Eldridge St
Very small space - 18 seats

name: Aquavit

walkIn.advice: Bar Room takes walk-ins and also accepts same-day reservations on Tock. Easier to get in than the Dining Room.

tips: Reservations for the Dining Room drop on the 1st of each month at 3:00 PM ET via Tock — be logged in and ready.
The Bar Room (prix-fixe or à la carte) is far easier to book and also takes walk-ins; good fallback if the Dining Room is sold out.
Two Michelin stars since 2017; the Chef's Tasting (longer) is the premium experience.
Arctic Bird's Nest dessert — goat cheese parfait with sea-buckthorn 'yolk' — is the signature finisher; don't skip it.
Parties over 6 must call directly; online Tock only serves up to 6 guests.
signatureDish: Arctic Bird's Nest (goat cheese parfait, sea-buckthorn yolk)

name: Aska
tips: Reservations for next month open on the 1st at noon ET sharp — set an alarm and use the direct OpenTable link, not the Aska website redirect.
All sales are final; no cancellations or reschedules, so only book a date you are certain about.
Closed Monday and Tuesday; Wednesday is the easiest night to land.
The wine pairing leans heavily Nordic and natural — ask for the non-alcoholic pairing if you want an equally thoughtful alternative.
Chef Fredrik Berselius sources from his own farm in the Hudson Valley; the menu shifts with harvest cycles, so dishes on social media may not reflect your actual meal.
signatureDish: Aged duck with fermented berries and pine

name: Atera
tips: Release batches drop in the early morning ET; set a Tock alert for your preferred date.
The $298 all-inclusive tasting menu covers even caviar and truffles — no supplement surprises.
Chef Ronny Emborg runs a 20-course Scandinavian-influenced menu that changes seasonally; go without dietary restrictions for the full experience.
Cancellations require one week's notice — plan accordingly.
Multiple seating types (chef's counter, formal dining, high-top) go live at different times; check all three pages on Tock for better odds.

name: Atoboy
tips: Resy drop at midnight ET, 29 days in advance — book the moment the window opens.
Prix-fixe format: each guest picks 3 dishes shared family-style; the menu rotates seasonally.
Cancellations surface fairly often — check Resy 1–2 days before your target date.
Easier than its sibling Atomix upstairs, but still competitive on weekend prime-time slots.
Book online up to 6 guests; larger parties need to contact the restaurant directly.
signatureDish: Aged Beef with Fermented Black Bean

name: Au Cheval

walkIn.advice: Downstairs bar is first-come first-served with the full menu. Bar seating upstairs is also walk-in. Both fill fast on weekends.

tips: Reservations open at 9 AM, 21 days out on Resy; a $2.50/person reservation fee applies (holiday fees may vary).
The bar and downstairs bar are walk-in only with the full menu — arrive before 6 PM on weeknights.
Online reservations limited to parties of 1–6; larger groups call ahead.
Located on Cortlandt Alley — a hidden cobblestone passage; GPS to 33 Cortlandt Alley.
The double cheeseburger with fried egg is non-negotiable; the bone marrow and crispy frites are equally essential.

name: Balthazar

walkIn.lineBy: N/A — arrive early for prime dinner seating
walkIn.advice: Breakfast and late-night (post-11 PM) walk-in slots are far more available than dinner. For dinner walk-ins, try arriving at 5:30 PM or after 10 PM.

tips: Reservations drop at midnight ET every Saturday for dates 30 days out — one of the only NYC Resy drops still at midnight; set an alarm.
Breakfast at Balthazar (starting 7:30 AM weekdays, 8:00 AM weekends) is the city's best walk-in meal — croissants, steak frites, and the full brasserie menu without the reservation battle.
The raw bar (oysters, plateau de fruits de mer) is the move for groups — order the large plateau before anything else.
Keith McNally's front-of-house staff are trained to seat regulars first in the prime center section; if you're a first-timer, confirm you want a table, not a banquette corner.
Late supper (after 11 PM) is underrated and nearly always walkable — same kitchen, less noise, the city crowd thins out.
signatureDish: Steak frites — bavette with Balthazar butter, pommes frites; also the plateau de fruits de mer for groups

name: Bird Dog

walkIn.advice: Pasta happy hour (4–6 PM) is walk-in only — half-price pasta at the bar, no reservations accepted for that window.

tips: 14-day Resy window with 9 AM drops — one of the more civilized release times in NYC, easier to hit than midnight spots.
Three menu paths: Family Style Chef's Tasting, Three Course Prix Fixe, or Fried Chicken Dinner — decide before you sit, the table commits together.
Pasta happy hour (4–6 PM) is walk-in bar only; half-price housemade pasta is a genuine steal.
Cancellation policy charges $20 per person within 6 hours — cancel early or you'll be charged.
Note: the chunk listed NoHo as the neighborhood, but the restaurant is at 525 Hudson St, firmly in the West Village.

name: Bonnie's

walkIn.advice: A few bar seats and one or two tables are held back for walk-ins nightly; worth attempting on a weeknight before 6 PM.

tips: Releases daily at 10 AM ET, 13 days out — from the restaurant's own policy page; be on Resy at 10:00 AM sharp.
Chef Calvin Eng named the restaurant after his mother — the menu is deeply personal Hong Kong-American nostalgia with NYC swagger; don't skip the McSorley's dark float dessert.
398 Manhattan Ave, Williamsburg — not easy walking from the L train; plan the G or a cab.
The char siu and har gow are required orders; the egg tart is a dessert that books out fast — ask your server to hold one.
Michelin Bib Gourmand — one of the better-value hard bookings in the city, with plates topping out around $40.

name: Buvette

walkIn.advice: Walk-ins are common and often successful — the bar fills quickly but tables turn fast. Best chance is off-peak hours or early evening.

tips: Reservations open 14 days out at 10am ET on Resy — not as competitive as harder spots but weekend evenings still move fast.
Walk-ins are a legitimate strategy; the room turns quickly and off-peak (before 6:30 PM or after 9 PM) is your best bet.
Located at 42 Grove Street in the West Village — the tight Parisian-style interior is part of the charm; expect cozy.
All-day service: excellent for brunch or a late glass of wine with charcuterie, not just dinner.
The croque-madame and avocado toast are brunch staples; at dinner, the braised meats and natural wine list are the focus.

name: Charlie Bird

walkIn.advice: Bar seats are walk-in only and a genuine option — the full menu is served there. Early Bird happy hour 5–6 PM at the bar is $10 drinks.

tips: 30-day Resy window with midnight drops — straightforward compared to other NYC hardcores, but weekend primetime still fills fast.
Bar and walk-in counter seats are your safety valve; arrive at 5 PM opening.
The farro with duck liver and the cacio e pepe are menu constants worth ordering.
Private events fill the back room frequently — if the Resy page shows limited slots, check if a buyout is blocking full availability.
For groups of 8+ a prix-fixe is required; contact info@charliebirdnyc.com in advance.
signatureDish: Farro with duck liver, radish, and herbs

name: Ci Siamo

walkIn.advice: The entire bar (13 stools) and lounge (~20 seats) are walk-in only — a reliable alternative to the dining room.

tips: Primary booking platform is SevenRooms (sevenrooms.com/reservations/cisiamo), not Resy — book there directly for best availability.
Reservations open at noon ET, 28 days in advance — be on SevenRooms at 12:00 PM sharp with date and party size selected.
Bar and lounge are entirely walk-in; 13 bar stools and ~20 lounge seats available throughout the evening.
1 Michelin Star; live-fire Italian cooking from Chef Hillary Sterling — the wood-grilled dishes are the move.
If dining room is sold out, check the bar seating or set a cancellation alert via SevenRooms.

name: Claud
tips: Reservations open 14 days in advance at 9 AM ET on Resy — one of the shorter windows in NYC so the crush is intense.
Michelin-starred wine bar from Chase Sinzer and Joshua Pinsky (both Momofuku Ko alums); the wine program is world-class.
The roasted chicken and pork chop with smoked onion jus are the main event proteins — order one.
Cancellations under 24 hours incur a $50/person fee; treat as a confirmed commitment.
Parties of 1–6; 2 hours allotted for most tables. Shows up on secondary market sites but prices are steep.

name: COQODAQ

walkIn.advice: Patio seats and some bar seats are held for walk-ins; arrive 15 minutes before opening and ask the host — lunch walk-ins are easier than dinner.

tips: 13-day window drops at 10 AM ET daily — be logged in and ready; Dining Hall, Dining Room, Patio, and Bar are all separate inventory pools, so check all seat types.
Gracious Hospitality Management (Simon Kim) concept — the room is a Rockwell Group cathedral; reserve a Dining Hall booth for the full theater.
Order the whole fried chicken (serves 2–3) plus the caviar service and the soju cocktails — the fried chicken alone is worth the hunt.
Lunch is significantly easier to book than dinner and the menu is largely identical — a good path in if evening slots are gone.
Michelin one star as of 2025; the wine list is built around high-acid whites that pair well with fried chicken — let the sommelier guide you.
signatureDish: Whole Korean fried chicken with caviar

name: Corner Bar

walkIn.advice: Bar seats are walk-in only. Arrive close to opening for the best odds; the bar is a genuine fallback.

tips: Despite a Resy page appearing in search, bookings are on OpenTable — use OpenTable directly.
Releases 30 days in advance at 5 AM ET on OpenTable; set an alarm.
The bar is walk-in and serves the full menu — a real option if you miss the online drop.
Inspired by classic European bistros; the steak frites and wine list punch above the price point.
Parties of 7–16 require email booking via info@cornerbarnyc.com.

name: Cosme

walkIn.advice: Bar seats and a small number of walk-in tables are held; arrive before opening and ask the host directly — weeknight early slots have the best odds.

tips: 28-day rolling window on Resy; no fixed public release time — check early in the day and activate Notify immediately if your slot is gone.
Bar seats are not on Resy — walk in or call; the bar menu is full-restaurant, not a limited version.
Corn husk meringue dessert always sells out — mention it to your server at the start of the meal to reserve one.
Weekday dinner seatings at 5:30–6 PM are the easiest to land; Friday/Saturday 7–9 PM is nearly impossible without a head start.
Chef Enrique Olvera and chef de cuisine Daniela Soto-Innes have both won James Beard awards — this is one of the most acclaimed Mexican restaurants outside Mexico City.
signatureDish: Corn husk meringue with corn mousse

name: Crown Shy

walkIn.advice: Bar seats (up to 2 people) and outdoor tables are walk-in only; show up at 5pm opening or check the bar mid-service for cancellations.

tips: Reservations drop daily at noon, 28 days in advance — set a phone alarm and book exactly at 12:00 PM ET.
Bar and outdoor seating are walk-in only; arriving right at 5pm opening is your best shot.
Enable Resy Notify for your target date as a backup; cancellations move quickly.
The dining room is inside a landmark 1932 Art Deco skyscraper — request a window table overlooking the lobby when booking.
1 Michelin Star; tasting menu and à la carte both available — the chicken dish is a consistent highlight.

name: Dame

walkIn.advice: Outdoor structure sometimes has walk-in availability; indoors is almost always fully reserved. Arrive at 5:30 PM opening.

tips: Reservations drop daily at noon, 21 days out — set a calendar reminder and book the moment they open.
Cancellations within 12 hours incur a $50 per person fee; take any Notify hit seriously.
Maximum party size is 6; groups of 7+ require a full restaurant buyout (up to 27 guests).
The fish and chips and the dressed crab are the signatures — don't over-order starters or you won't make it to the mains.
Monday is closed; Tuesday is the easiest booking night.

name: Daniel

walkIn.advice: Bar and lounge seats are first-come first-served; arrive at opening for the best shot.

tips: Reservations open exactly 60 days out on Resy — set a calendar reminder and hit it at 10 AM ET.
The bar and lounge take walk-ins with the full menu; arrive at 5:30 PM for best odds.
Ask for a table in the main dining room rotunda, not the side alcove — the room itself is part of the experience.
The prix-fixe is required at dinner; order the foie gras torchon if it's on the menu.
Cancellations often surface 1–3 days before; check Resy app in the evenings.

name: Dhamaka
tips: Releases on SevenRooms 14 days in advance at 9 AM ET — be logged in and refreshing before 9.
Parties of 7+ must contact for private dining; online booking caps at 6.
One of the hardest reservations in NYC — check cancellations on SevenRooms at off-peak hours.
The menu focuses on regional Indian cooking rarely found in the US; whole roasted meats and offal are the point.
Go with a group of 4–6 to cover the most of the menu.

name: Eleven Madison Park
tips: Reservations open on the 1st of the prior month at 10 AM ET sharp — have Resy loaded and ready at 9:55 AM.
The menu is no longer exclusively vegan; at booking you can select a fully plant-based or hybrid menu that includes duck, scallops, or lobster.
Join the Resy waitlist immediately if your date is full — cancellations are common and the system notifies you instantly.
Bar seats at Clemente Bar (downstairs) are easier to snag and do not require the full tasting menu commitment.
Tasting menu is ~$365 per person before drinks; gratuity is included.
signatureDish: Three-day beet prepared in clay pot

name: Estela

walkIn.advice: Walk-in bar seating available nightly. Arrive before doors open and wait at the bar; seats fill quickly but turnover happens.

tips: Resy drop is daily at 9 AM ET, 28 days out — use the app with notifications on.
Bar seats are walk-in only and worth pursuing; the full menu is served there.
Lunch on Fri–Sun is dramatically easier to book than dinner — same kitchen, far fewer people chasing it.
One Michelin star; the menu is share-focused, order 3–4 plates for two.
Cancellations surface throughout the week — check Resy daily if you missed the drop.

name: Foxface Natural

walkIn.advice: Counter seats for parties of 1-3 often available walk-in; arrive early as the space is small.

tips: No reservations by phone or email — Resy only; the restaurant will text/email only for existing booking help.
Counter seats (1-3 people) are the easiest walk-in option; show up within the first 30 minutes of service.
Menu driven by wild fish, game, and seasonal forage from a wood-burning kitchen — the menu changes frequently.
Set a Resy Notify alert; this is a small room and cancellations do come up.
They will offer your seats to the waitlist 15 minutes after your reservation time — do not be late.

name: Frenchette

walkIn.advice: Bar, banquettes, and the outdoor structure are your best walk-in bets. Aim to arrive before 5:30 PM on a weeknight — weekend walk-ins are brutal.

tips: Reservations drop 30 days out at midnight; about 80% of the room releases then, with a second trickle a couple days before the date.
600–800 people are on the Notify List on a given night — enable Resy Notify immediately after midnight misses.
The bar seats are first-come first-served and are genuinely great for the full menu.
Order the foie gras torchon and the rotisserie duck — both are consistent anchors regardless of season.
If you email reservations@frenchettenyc.com for a specific occasion you sometimes surface cancelled tables not on Resy.

name: Frevo
tips: Reservations for next month open on the 1st at 2 PM ET on Tock — the 16-seat counter sells out in minutes.
The art gallery entrance at 48 W 8th St is easy to miss; confirm the address before your visit.
Menu, gallery exhibition, and wine pairings rotate every four months — check Instagram to see the current chapter before booking.
No shorts or flip-flops; elegant casual is enforced at the door.
A deposit is required at booking — Tock charges the full ticket price upfront, not just a hold.

name: Gabriel Kreuther
tips: Book exactly 28 days out on Tock at 10 AM ET — the main dining room fills within minutes of the drop.
Resy also lists Gabriel Kreuther but the primary/preferred platform is Tock; use Tock for best availability.
If dinner is sold out, the Bar Room has a separate, less competitive reservation track — still on Tock.
The champagne and caviar service (Tue–Sat 4–9 PM) has more availability and is a worthy standalone experience.
Two Michelin stars; jacket preferred for the main dining room.

name: Gramercy Tavern

walkIn.advice: The Tavern (front room) is walk-in only with no reservations — arrive before 5:30 PM opening to join the line; it fills within the first 30 minutes. The bar stools are your best solo bet.

tips: Dining Room reservations open at noon ET exactly 28 days in advance — set a Resy alert for your target date and be ready at 11:58 AM.
The Tavern (front room) is walk-in only and easier to access; quality and kitchen are identical — not a consolation prize.
Tasting menu in the Dining Room is prix-fixe only; the Tavern menu is à la carte and more casual.
Cancellations surface frequently on Resy — enable Notify and check back a few days before your target date.
One of the few fine-dining rooms that's genuinely family-friendly; no hard dress code enforced.
signatureDish: Smoked duck breast with celery root and foie gras torchon

name: i Sodi

walkIn.advice: Walk-ins are always welcome per the restaurant — a small number of unreserved seats at the bar and occasional early tables are available; arrive at opening.

tips: Midnight ET release, 13 days out — one of the very few NYC spots still dropping at midnight; be on Resy at exactly 12:00 AM, not 12:01.
Inventory clears in 1–2 seconds for prime slots; have party size, date, and credit card pre-loaded before the clock ticks over.
Rita Sodi's Tuscan cucina povera is the whole point — order the pappardelle al cinghiale, ribollita if on the menu, and the tiramisu.
The restaurant is physically small (~40 seats); any no-show or cancellation resurfaces quickly — check Resy at 10 AM and 5 PM on your target date.
Sister restaurant Buvette (next door, French) books much more easily and shares a similar sensibility — a real fallback.

name: Indian Accent

walkIn.advice: Walk-ins are accommodated when space permits. Call ahead on the day for best odds.

tips: Parties of 4 or fewer book online via OpenTable; groups of 5+ must call 212-842-8070.
The tasting menu is the way to go — the blue cheese naan and makhan malai are standout courses.
Sister to the Delhi original; same creative Indian fine dining DNA with seasonal NYC produce.
Walk-ins are welcome if space allows — lunch is easier than dinner for drop-ins.
Located at 123 W 56th St steps from Carnegie Hall; convenient for pre-show dining.
signatureDish: Blue cheese naan with makhan malai

name: Ito
tips: Reservations open at 12 PM ET exactly 21 days out on Resy — noon is unusual, so most competitors miss it.
The restaurant is in Tribeca (75 Barclay St), not Williamsburg — the data hint was wrong.
Two seatings per night, Tuesday–Saturday: 6:00 PM and 8:45 PM at a 16-seat counter.
$295 per person; cancellations within 48 hours are fully non-refundable.
Chefs Masashi Ito and Kevin Kim blend Japanese technique with Korean and American influences — a genuinely distinct omakase voice in the city.
signatureDish: Seasonal omakase counter with Japanese-Korean technique

name: Jean-Georges
tips: Tock is the primary platform; also mirrored on Resy and OpenTable — book via Tock for widest availability.
Tue–Sat only; lunch is significantly easier to book than dinner and offers better value on the three-course prix-fixe ($138 vs. $218 tasting).
Two Michelin stars as of 2025 — not the hardest reservation in the city, so 2–3 weeks out is often enough with flexibility on time.
Request a window table overlooking Central Park when booking — stock phrase in the notes field on Tock.
Jackets suggested; t-shirts, shorts, and sportswear are declined — dress code is lightly enforced but real.
signatureDish: Egg caviar (scrambled egg with caviar in a shell)

name: Jua
tips: 21-day Resy window, midnight drops — two seatings per night (6 PM and 8:30 PM), Tuesday through Sunday only.
Seven-course tasting menu at $150 per person — one of the better-value Michelin tasting menus in the city.
The kitchen cannot accommodate vegan, vegetarian, dairy-free, gluten-free, no-seafood, no-sesame, or no-allium diets — check before booking.
Phone calls only answered 2–6 PM; for fastest response email info@juanyc.com.
Late arrivals of 15+ minutes risk losing the table — allow extra transit time.

name: Keens Steakhouse

walkIn.advice: Bar and the Pub Room accept walk-ins; arrive at 5:00 PM for the best chance at bar seating before the after-work Midtown crowd arrives.

tips: The mutton chop (technically lamb saddle) is the non-negotiable order — it's the dish that made Keens famous since 1885.
The Pipe Club room and the Lincoln Room are the best private dining spaces; request specifically when booking groups.
Resy releases at 10 AM ET 30 days in advance; weekend prime time (6:30–8 PM) books out fast, but Tuesday–Thursday stays bookable days out.
The clay pipe collection covering the ceiling is legitimate history — arrive early and look up; each pipe belonged to a historical member (Babe Ruth, Teddy Roosevelt, etc.).
Single diners at the bar get full menu access and often chat with regulars who know the older servers by name — one of the better solo dining experiences in the city.
signatureDish: Mutton chop — slow-roasted lamb saddle chop, served whole, for one

name: Kono
tips: 14-seat counter in Canal Arcade (between Bowery and Elizabeth St) — it's a hidden passage, so know the entrance before you go.
Closed Sundays; runs six nights per week with a 16-course binchotan-grilled omakase ($185/pp).
Cancellations must be made 48 hours in advance — no-shows are noticed in a 14-seat room.
Holds three Michelin stars; despite the difficulty hint of 3, this is one of NYC's most decorated spots — book as early as the window allows.
Check Resy at midnight when the new day opens; also monitor for cancellations mid-week.

name: Kosaka
tips: Tock is the only booking channel — no Resy or OpenTable; Maki Kosaka (sister spot) is on Resy and is a lower-stakes fallback.
Sushi bar ($295 pp) seats you in front of Chef Soma; dining table ($270 pp) is slightly more relaxed — both are full Edomae omakase.
18-seat room means any night can feel fully booked weeks out; lunch has no service, so dinner only — target Sunday or Monday for slightly softer demand.
Fish is flown daily from Toyosu Market in Tokyo; the menu changes entirely with season and market — ask the chef about the day's sourcing.
Cancellation penalty of $150/pp kicks in inside 48 hours — set a reminder to reschedule early if plans change.
signatureDish: Hokkaido uni with bafun and murasaki

name: l'abeille

walkIn.advice: Bar seats are available for à la carte ordering without the full tasting menu — best shot at a walk-in; arrive before doors open.

tips: Reservations open at midnight 21 days out on Resy — set a phone alarm.
The six-course chef's tasting menu is the main event; the bar offers à la carte seasonal dishes if you can't get a table.
Chef Mitsunobu Nagae trained in Tokyo and Paris — Japanese technique in French form; expect subtle, precise flavors.
The sister wine bar l'abeille à côté next door is far easier to walk into and shares the same kitchen sensibility.
Closed Sunday and Monday.
signatureDish: Six-course chef's tasting menu with French-Japanese seasonal compositions

name: L'Artusi

walkIn.advice: Bar and a few walk-in seats available nightly — arrive right at opening and ask the host; the bar is a full experience, not a consolation prize.

tips: 14-day rolling window drops at 9 AM ET every morning — be logged into Resy with card on file before 9 AM.
228 W 10th St is the West Village's most sought-after Italian; prime Fri/Sat 7–8 PM slots go in under a minute.
Bar stools along the open kitchen are not on Resy — walk in or call after 2 PM the same day.
The cacio e pepe and black truffle tagliatelle are the non-negotiable orders; the wine list skews natural Italian at fair markups.
Larger parties (6–8) must call; Resy only handles up to 5 online — email info@lartusi.com with dates for groups.

name: Laser Wolf

walkIn.advice: Walk-ins are possible at the bar or for early/late dinner slots. Come in the first 30 minutes after opening for best odds.

tips: Resy drop at 10 AM ET, 21 days in advance — tables sell out in minutes; be in the app right at 10.
Rooftop on The Hoxton, Williamsburg — ask for an outdoor table when booking for Manhattan skyline views.
The salatim (salad spread) arrives automatically to start; the whole-roasted chicken is the anchor entrée.
Michael Solomonov's flagship NYC outpost; the coal-fired pita with hummus is mandatory.
Cancellations are frequent — check Resy the day before and the morning of your desired date.

name: Le Coucou
tips: Midnight ET release — one of the few high-end NYC spots that still drops at 12 AM; be on Resy at exactly midnight.
28-day rolling window; mark your calendar 28 days before your target date.
Cancellations surface regularly — set Resy Notify and check mid-week around 5–7 PM ET when people cancel prior-week bookings.
Counter seats by the open kitchen are held back and release the day-of — worth checking Resy after 10 AM.
One Michelin star; chef Daniel Rose's classic French repertoire makes this a destination meal — order the moules marinières and any whole fish.

name: Le Jardinier

walkIn.advice: Bar seats are available without a reservation; arrive at opening for the best selection.

tips: Books via SevenRooms (primary) and OpenTable; the restaurant site links SevenRooms directly.
Chef Alain Verzeroli's vegetable-forward Michelin-starred menu changes seasonally — check the current menu before going.
Lunch is easier to book than dinner and offers good value with the prix-fixe.
Located in the SELENE skyscraper on Lexington Ave — entrance on 58th St.
If dinner is full, call directly at (212) 451-9211; they occasionally hold tables for phone reservations.

name: Le Pavillon

walkIn.advice: Bar seating is available walk-in, subject to availability. Arrive early in the evening for best odds.

tips: Releases 60 days in advance at midnight ET on Resy — set an alarm and book the moment the window opens.
Bar walk-ins are a genuine option here; the full menu is available at the bar.
Daniel Boulud's vegetable- and seafood-focused menu changes seasonally — the tasting menu is the move.
Located on the 24th floor of One Vanderbilt with views of Grand Central; ask for a window seat when booking.
Closed Sundays; lunch service runs weekdays only.
signatureDish: Roasted Beet & Citrus with Whipped Goat Cheese

name: Le Rock

walkIn.advice: Bar and counter seats available walk-in; arrive at opening (11 AM weekdays, 4 PM Saturday). Lunch is less competitive than dinner.

tips: Reservations open 30 days in advance on Resy — check at 9 AM ET for new slots.
From chefs Lee Hanson and Riad Nasr (Frenchette); the bison au poivre and escargot are must-orders.
Lunch (Mon–Fri) is dramatically easier to book than dinner; nearly the full menu is available.
The profiterole with caramel ice cream and warm chocolate sauce is a signature dessert worth saving room for.
Closed Sundays; Saturday is dinner only from 4 PM.

name: Manhatta

walkIn.advice: Bar seats and late-evening slots (after 9 PM) are easier to walk into; early dinner on weekdays is most accessible.

tips: Reservations open at noon exactly 28 days out via SevenRooms — the restaurant also appears on Resy but SevenRooms is the primary platform.
60th-floor views are the main draw; request a window table when booking or call ahead.
Weekday lunch is far easier to book than weekend dinner.
The bar menu is available all day — if dinner is full, grab a drink and snack with the same view.
Late slots (9:30 PM+) often open up same-day on the app.

name: Marea
tips: Book exactly 30 days out at 9 AM on Resy — weekend dinner slots disappear within minutes.
The lunch service is significantly easier to book and offers the same kitchen quality at lower prices.
Sit at the bar for a more relaxed pace; the pasta bar area books separately on Resy.
The fusilli with red wine-braised octopus and bone marrow is the must-order; don't skip the crudo to start.
Valet parking is available on Central Park South — confirm when booking if needed.
signatureDish: Fusilli with red wine-braised octopus and bone marrow

name: Mari
tips: Reservations drop at midnight 30 days out on Resy — set a phone alarm and be ready.
$145 per person seasonal tasting menu; handrolls feature uni, wagyu, toro, and caviar.
From the team behind Kochi — serious Korean fine dining pedigree, not a gimmick spot.
The sister concept Mari.ne Handroll (also on Resy) is a slightly easier get and shares the same culinary DNA.
Michelin-starred; book well in advance for Friday and Saturday evenings.
signatureDish: Korean handroll omakase with wagyu, uni, and caviar

name: Masa
tips: Reservations drop on Tock on the 1st of each month for the following month — be on the page at 9am ET sharp.
Two experiences: standard omakase ($750pp) and Hinoki Counter ($950pp); the counter faces Chef Masa directly.
Lunch omakase is abbreviated and slightly easier to book than dinner.
Cancellations occasionally surface mid-month; set a Tock alert and check weekly.
Dress the occasion — this is a 3-Michelin-star room; no shorts or sneakers.

name: Minetta Tavern

walkIn.doors: Open daily from 5 PM (lunch Wed–Fri, brunch weekends)
walkIn.advice: Bar seating is first-come, first-served every night. Arrive by 5 PM for a realistic chance. Shoulder-to-shoulder but fully in the room.

tips: Reservations drop at midnight on Resy exactly 30 days out — slots for Friday/Saturday vanish in seconds.
Bar seats are walk-in only; arrive right at 5 PM opening for the best chance, especially weeknights.
The Black Label Burger (dry-aged blend, caramelized onions, brioche) is the most-discussed dish — always order it.
Michelin one-star; the room is gorgeous and the bone marrow is a required starter alongside the burger.
Lunch (Wed–Fri) and weekend brunch are easier to book than dinner; same kitchen, less competition.

name: Misi

walkIn.advice: Bar and counter seats are walk-in. Arrive at opening (5:30 PM) for best shot — the bar fills immediately.

tips: Resy drop is daily at 10 AM ET, 27 days out — not the often-cited 28; act precisely at 10.
Bar walk-ins at opening are a reliable backup — arrive by 5:15 PM.
Missy Robbins' pasta-focused menu; order the aged ricotta with honey as a starter.
Cancellations are common the week of — check Resy at noon and again at 7 PM.
Located on the Williamsburg waterfront; outdoor seating in warm months has more walk-in flexibility.

name: Nakaji
tips: Only 10 counter seats; seatings at 5:30 PM and 8 PM — pick your seating when booking on Resy.
Reservations open 14 days out at midnight on Resy; set an alarm and be ready at 12:00 AM.
Phone reservations available Tue–Sun if Resy slots are gone: (646) 478-8282.
Omakase runs approximately 2.5 hours; plan the night around it.
Also listed on Tock but Resy is the active platform — use Resy.

name: Naks
tips: Books via SevenRooms (not Resy) — 7 days in advance at 9 AM ET. The hint lists Resy but SevenRooms is the live platform.
18-course kamayan tasting menu by chef Eric Valdez (Unapologetic Foods); this is a full-commitment dinner.
The ginataang alimango (Dungeness crab in coconut milk, chile, ginger, shrimp paste) is the standout course.
Closed Mondays; Tue–Fri dinner only, Sat–Sun brunch + dinner. Cancellations under 24 hours = $40/person fee.
A la carte is also available as an alternative to the tasting menu — good if the full kamayan feels like too much.
signatureDish: Ginataang alimango (Dungeness crab in coconut-chile broth)

name: Noda
tips: Reservations release in batches on Tock — recent drops have been around the 21st of the month at 3 PM ET; check Tock for the exact next release date.
Fully prepaid at $400 per person; Tock charges at booking, no refunds.
Parties of 1 to 6 all seated at the single counter — every seat faces the chef.
The Japanese whiskey collection is one of the most extensive in NYC; budget extra for pours.
Chef Tsunoda trained in edomae in Ginza — the pacing is unhurried, plan 2.5–3 hours.

name: Noz 17
tips: Platform is SevenRooms — not Tock; book via noz.global/reserve-seats or the link in their Instagram bio.
Only 7 counter seats; two seatings nightly at 6 PM and 9 PM, Monday–Saturday — availability is extremely thin.
Email oshinobi@noz17.com directly for cancellation list; the team responds and occasionally releases single seats this way.
The 20–25 course Edomae-style menu is fully chef-driven with no substitutions; dietary restrictions must be disclosed well in advance.
Sushi Noz (UES) and Noz Market are sister restaurants on the same SevenRooms system — if Noz 17 is sold out, check noz.global for group availability.
signatureDish: Chef's seasonal Edomae nigiri progression — aged hirame and otoro are recurring highlights

name: Odo
tips: Reservations release monthly via Tock in batches — check the Tock page for the exact drop date (typically mid-month for the following month).
2 Michelin Stars; hyper-seasonal Japanese kaiseki-influenced tasting menu by Chef Hiroki Odo.
Cannot accommodate vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, or guests who cannot eat seafood/seaweed — confirm dietary restrictions before booking.
Odo Lounge next door has a cocktail program and a more accessible entry point; also on Tock.
Two dinner seatings: 6:00 PM and 8:30 PM — the earlier seating is marginally easier to book.

name: Oiji Mi

walkIn.advice: Bar and lounge seats: majority held as walk-in first-come first-served. Arrive at 6 PM opening. À la carte menu only at bar — not the prix-fixe.

tips: Dining room releases 30 days in advance at midnight ET on Resy; 5-course prix-fixe at $150/person.
Bar/lounge is mostly walk-in (FCFS) with à la carte — great for a spontaneous meal without the tasting menu commitment.
Michelin one-star; chef Brian Kim's chili lobster ramyun is the dish everyone talks about.
Striped jack hwe (crudo) with seaweed scallion vinaigrette is an underrated opener — order it in the dining room.
Lounge reservations are also available on Resy with less competition than the dining room.

name: One White Street

walkIn.advice: Ground-floor wine bar operates as walk-in; the 8-course tasting menu upstairs is reservation-only. The wine bar is a legitimate entry point and serves food.

tips: Two distinct spaces: walk-in wine bar on the ground floor, ticketed 8-course tasting menu ($185 pp) on the upper floors — book the tasting menu via Resy, wine bar is first-come.
Friday and Sunday lunch (recently added) is dramatically easier to book than dinner — same kitchen, same produce, lower competition.
The restaurant sources from its own rooftop farm in Tribeca; menu shifts weekly based on harvest — check their Instagram before going to see what's in.
Chef Austin Johnson's wine list is one of the best in NYC natural wine; lean on the sommelier for pairings rather than doing the add-on pairing package.
The townhouse has a residential feel — show up a few minutes early and take in the ground floor before heading upstairs.
signatureDish: Dry-aged beef with rooftop farm vegetables — the preparation shifts seasonally but anchors the menu

name: Oxomoco

walkIn.advice: The sunlit, plant-filled dining room has some walk-in capacity, especially for brunch and early weeknight dinner. Weekend dinner walk-ins are very difficult.

tips: Resy is the booking platform; weekend dinner reservations are highly competitive — book the day the 30-day window opens.
Currently running a seasonal seafood pop-up concept 'Playa Oxomoco' — the menu is more seafood-forward than usual; confirm the current format before visiting.
The wood-fired cooking is the soul of the menu — the smoked beet tostada and any wood-roasted protein are the moves.
Brunch and lunch are meaningfully easier to book than dinner; great for a first visit.
Groups of 8+ must email hola@oxomoconyc.com — Resy won't surface large-party slots.

name: Pasquale Jones

walkIn.advice: Bar and patio seats are available for walk-ins; show up at opening or between 8–9 PM when early diners turn over.

tips: Booking window is 45 days — longer than most assume; book five to six weeks out for weekend nights.
The wood-fired clam pie with lardo is the must-order; don't overlook the pastas.
Walk-in bar seating is genuinely available on weeknights — arrive at opening or after 8:30 PM when the first wave clears.
Lunch and brunch (Wed–Sun) are dramatically easier to walk into than dinner.
From the Delicious Hospitality Group (also Charlie Bird, Pasquale Slice) — the team knows hospitality.

name: Pastis

walkIn.advice: The zinc bar and cafe tables near the front are typically first-come; arrive 15 minutes before your desired meal time on weekdays for the best shot.

tips: 28-day rolling window on Resy; Starr Restaurants property — not the hardest in the city but Saturday dinner and Sunday brunch are legitimately difficult.
Breakfast (Mon–Fri 8–11 AM) and weekday lunch are easy walk-ins — the zinc bar vibe is best experienced without the reservation chase anyway.
Outdoor terrace tables on Gansevoort Street release the same window but move faster in summer — check 'Outdoor' seat type separately on Resy.
Classic brasserie menu: order the steak frites (black angus), croque madame at lunch, and profiteroles to finish.
The late-night bar scene (Fri/Sat past 11 PM) is effectively walk-in territory — the restaurant stops seating new dinner parties but the bar stays open.

name: Per Se
tips: Reservations drop on the 1st of each month at 2:00 PM ET for the following month — be logged in to Tock and ready at 1:58 PM.
Both the 9-course chef's tasting ($425 pp) and the vegetable tasting menu must be prepaid in full at booking; cancellations are strictly enforced.
Request a window table overlooking Central Park when booking — specify in the notes field; it makes a material difference.
If the 1st is sold out, check back on Tock daily — cancellations surface in real time and the 30-day window still applies.
Dress code is business casual enforced; jackets recommended for men.
signatureDish: Oysters and Pearls — sabayon of pearl tapioca with Island Creek oysters and caviar

name: Peter Luger

walkIn.advice: Walk-ins accepted at lunch until 2 PM. Bar seating and singles have best luck noon–4 PM on weekdays.

tips: Lunch walk-ins are viable on weekdays; arrive as soon as they open at noon for the best shot.
Peter Luger does NOT accept credit cards — bring cash, a US debit card, or the Luger house card.
Order the porterhouse for two minimum; the extra thick bacon appetizer and sliced tomato with onion are non-negotiable starters.
Phone reservations are also accepted at 718-387-7400 — sometimes easier than Resy for large parties.
Stick to the classics; the menu rarely changes and the staff prefers it that way.

name: Raf's

walkIn.advice: Bar seats are held for walk-ins nightly — your best bet if you can't snag a table on Resy.

tips: Reservations drop at 9 AM sharp, 14 days out on Resy — set an alarm and move fast for weekends.
The bar walk-in is a legitimate strategy and often the better seat — you see the room and the pasta comes out the same.
All-day bakery (open from 9 AM) for croissants and pastries requires no reservation.
From the Musket Room team; the French-Italian pastas and roast chicken are the anchors of the menu.
Closed Monday.
signatureDish: French-Italian seasonal pasta and whole roasted chicken

name: Raoul's

walkIn.advice: Walk-in bar seats available from 5 PM; arrive early as the bar fills fast. SoHo institution since 1975 — the scene at the bar is half the point.

tips: Reservations open at 8 AM sharp, 30 days out on Resy — set an alarm.
The bar is walk-in and worth it; shows up around 5–5:30 PM for a stool before the rush.
Open since 1975 — a genuine NYC institution, not a trend restaurant. Order the steak au poivre.
Weekend brunch (11 AM–2:30 PM) has a separate and slightly easier booking track.
Cancellations appear frequently on Resy the week of; check 2–3 days ahead in the evenings.

name: Rezdôra

walkIn.advice: A few bar seats are available walk-in; arrive right at opening (5 PM) for the best chance — the bar fills fast.

tips: Reservations drop at midnight exactly 29 days out — set an alarm; they go within minutes.
Resy allows bookings for up to 4 guests; larger groups need to call directly.
Bar seats walk-in only — show up at 5 PM sharp on a weeknight for the best odds.
The hand-rolled pasta is the whole point: order the tortellini in brodo and the tagliatelle al ragù.
Watch Resy cancellations at 4 PM the day before — confirmations go out and dropped slots reappear.

name: Rubirosa

walkIn.lineBy: No formal line; arrive at 11 AM or 5 PM opening for best walk-in odds
walkIn.advice: Bar seats are walk-in only. Peak waits run 1–2 hours on weekends; weekday lunch is most forgiving.

tips: Resy opens 7 days in advance at midnight ET sharp — weekend prime times vanish in seconds, so be ready at 11:59 PM.
A secondary dump of cancellations typically hits 25–26 hours before service time; worth checking the app.
The thin-crust Pappalardo family pizza is the draw; the vodka sauce and clam pie are the top orders.
Parties of 2 get 75 minutes; parties of 4–7 get 90 minutes — don't be late.
Walk-in bar seats are viable on weekday lunches; evenings and weekends require patience.

name: Saga
tips: Reservations drop on the 1st of each month for the following month on Resy — platform switched from Tock in 2024, confirm current platform before booking.
$100 per-person deposit required at booking; non-refundable within 48 hours of the reservation.
Located on the 63rd floor of 70 Pine — arrive 10 minutes early to clear building security and reach the elevator bank.
Wednesday–Thursday seatings are significantly easier to land than Friday–Saturday; the 4-course menu at $198 pp (Sun–Thu) offers better value than the 7-course.
The Terrace has a separate Resy listing (saga-the-lounge-and-terraces) and is easier to book if you want a taste of the space.
signatureDish: Dry-aged duck — preparations change seasonally but the duck has anchored the tasting menu since opening

name: Sailor

walkIn.advice: Over half the seats are held for walk-ins nightly; arrive 4:30–4:45 PM before the 5 PM opening to secure a spot.

tips: Official reservation platform is OpenTable — released at 11am, 14 days in advance (not Resy despite appearing there).
More than half the restaurant is walk-in only; showing up at 4:30–4:45 PM before the 5 PM open is a reliable strategy.
Prime 6–8 PM slots vanish within 90 minutes of the 11am drop — book immediately or go walk-in.
Outdoor seats released same-day based on weather forecast — check OpenTable the morning of.
Chef April Bloomfield helms the kitchen; the smash burger and roast chicken are must-orders.

name: Saint Theo's

walkIn.advice: Check Resy at 4 PM the evening before — text confirmations go out and released slots reappear. Also try the bar walk-in.

tips: Reservations open at 10 AM, 21 days in advance on Resy — no phone or email bookings accepted.
Best cancellation window: check Resy at 4 PM the day before, when the restaurant texts to reconfirm and dropped slots resurface.
Located at 340 Bleecker St in the West Village — note the chunk_3 hint said Williamsburg, but the restaurant is in the West Village.
Fresh seafood, handmade pasta, and artisan pizza; order the wood-fired dishes for the full experience.
Weekend brunch (11 AM–3 PM) books out nearly as fast as dinner; same 21-day window applies.

name: Sartiano's

walkIn.advice: Bar is first-come, first-served. The bar scene is part of the draw; arrive early evening for a seat.

tips: Dinner reservations are primarily handled by phone or email — call (212) 658-1155 between 10 AM–5 PM, or email reservations@sartianos.com.
A Resy page exists but dinner isn't reliably bookable there; use the phone/email channel.
Located inside The Mercer hotel at 99 Prince St; celebrity-magnet SoHo scene, dress accordingly.
Culinary direction by three-time James Beard winner Alfred Portale — the pasta and Dover sole are signatures.
One month advance is the practical window; call the morning you're 30 days out for prime slots.

name: Scarpetta

walkIn.advice: Bar seating at Hotel Seville's bar area is available walk-in; the dining room is reservation-heavy on weekends.

tips: Located inside Hotel Seville at 88 Madison Ave (NoMad) — neighborhood is technically NoMad, not Flatiron, despite some listings.
Resy drop is at 9 AM ET 30 days in advance; weekend prime-time slots (7–8 PM Friday/Saturday) go within minutes.
The spaghetti al pomodoro and tagliatelle with Bolognese are the two dishes to order regardless of anything else on the menu.
Pasta is handmade daily — the kitchen is at its most engaged Tuesday–Thursday; weekends can feel like a volume operation.
SevenRooms widget also exists on their site but Resy is the primary channel for dining room reservations.
signatureDish: Spaghetti al pomodoro — the defining dish; simple, precise, irreproducible at home

name: Semma

walkIn.advice: 12 bar seats held for walk-ins; queue by 4 PM for best odds. Staff takes a waitlist by text once bar is full — give your number and stay nearby.

tips: 14-day window releases daily at 9 AM ET on SevenRooms — most slots clear within 10–35 seconds; have your party size and preferred dates pre-loaded.
Wednesday 3-top at 9 PM has historically been the slowest-selling slot (~145 sec) — most other combos vanish in under 30 seconds.
Walk-in bar seats are a real path in; arrive by 4 PM and ask to be on the walk-in list — weeknights give better odds than weekends.
Chef Vijay Kumar's menu is Tamil Nadu-rooted: order the gunpowder dosa, wild boar curry, and any daily special fish preparation.
Unapologetic Foods group (Dhamaka, Adda) — if Semma is full, Dhamaka on Resy is slightly easier and equally exciting.

name: Shukette

walkIn.doors: Mon–Fri 5 PM, Sat 5 PM, Sun 4 PM
walkIn.advice: The 23-seat wrap-around concrete counter is reserved almost entirely for walk-ins. Arrive 30–45 min before opening to be in the first seating wave. Zero wait if you're first in line.

tips: Reservations drop at 10 AM ET daily on Resy — 30-day window, prime evening slots gone in seconds.
Counter walk-in is genuinely a good strategy: line up 30–45 min before opening and you'll get in at first seating.
Order the Frena bread and Not Your Average Hummus immediately — the bread is the signature item.
Chef Ayesha Nurdjaja's charcoal-grilled skewers are the main event; the cauliflower and lamb chops are standouts.
From the Cookshop/Shuka group; similar food DNA to Shuka but louder, more casual, and harder to book.

name: St. Anselm

walkIn.advice: The 11-seat bar and a quarter of the dining room are held for walk-ins nightly; call ahead at (718) 384-5054 to check availability.

tips: Reservations drop at midnight ET on Resy, 28 days in advance — set an alarm for 12:00 AM if you want a weekend prime-time slot.
11-seat bar and ~25% of dining room tables are walk-in; arrive before 5:30 PM for the best shot on weekends.
Call ahead to check walk-in availability — the staff is responsive at (718) 384-5054.
Menu is wood-fire focused — the hanger steak and bone-in chicken are the signatures; go with whichever the server emphasizes that night.
Max party size is 8 in the dining room; larger groups should inquire about private dining.

name: Sushi Nakazawa
tips: Dinner reservations open 30 days in advance; lunch opens 14 days out — lunch is dramatically easier to land.
Dinner seating is parties of 2 only; lunch accommodates 1–4 guests.
Three dinner seatings: 5 PM, 7:15 PM, 9:30 PM — the 9:30 seating is the last to fill.
The 20-course omakase is fully scripted by Chef Nakazawa; do not attempt to substitute or modify.
Book via Resy only — no OpenTable, no phone reservations.

name: Sushi Noz
tips: Reservations release approximately six weeks out on Tock; set a calendar alert and check every morning near the six-week mark.
Four seatings per night across two counters (6-seat at 5:45 PM / 8:45 PM, 8-seat at 6:00 PM / 9:00 PM) — the 6-seat counter is the more intimate experience.
No cancellations or rescheduling within 72 hours; full deposit forfeiture. Know your schedule before booking.
The omakase is hyper-seasonal Edomae-style — trust the chef's progression and avoid requests.
Sister spots Noz 17 and Noz Market are accessible fallbacks if Sushi Noz is sold out.

name: Tempura Matsui
tips: America's only Michelin-starred tempura counter — reservations are mandatory, zero walk-ins.
Book via Tock up to 30 days out; the counter is tiny (under 10 seats) so dinner slots disappear fast.
Dinner omakase is $295/person; lunch (Fri–Sat only) is $168/person — lunch is easier to book.
Request counter seats when booking to watch the chef work; the bar-side view is the full experience.
Cancellation policy: full charge applies for cancellations after June 2026 cutoffs — treat this like a ticketed event.

name: The Grill
tips: Reservations open at 10 AM sharp, 30 days in advance on Resy.
Dress code is enforced: no shorts, open-toed shoes, tank tops, athletic wear, or baseball caps.
The room — Mies van der Rohe's Seagram Building — is as important as the food; ask for the main dining room, not the bar.
Cancellation deadline for dinner is noon on the day of the reservation.
Lunch on weekdays is dramatically easier to book than weekend dinner.

name: The Modern

walkIn.advice: The Bar Room accepts walk-ins across all four seating areas and also takes reservations — easier entry point than the Dining Room.

tips: Dining Room reservations open 28 days out at noon ET on Resy — the Bar Room is also reservable and easier to snag.
The Bar Room is à la carte and walk-in friendly; an excellent alternative if the Dining Room is full.
2 Michelin Stars as of 2025; request a table overlooking MoMA's Abby Aldrich Rockefeller Sculpture Garden.
The Kitchen Table (up to 4 guests) offers a front-row kitchen experience — book separately through the restaurant directly.
Lunch tasting menu is harder to find guidance on but releases on the same 28-day window.

name: Yoshino
tips: Reservations drop at noon ET exactly 30 days out on Tock — prepayment required in full at booking, so have your card ready.
Two seatings: 5:30 PM and 8:00 PM, Monday–Saturday; the later seating often lingers slightly longer and the 8 PM slot is marginally easier to snag.
Add yourself to the Tock waitlist the moment your target date sells out — single-seat cancellations appear regularly.
At ~$648 all-in (meal + service + tax), it's one of NYC's pricier omakases — the price reflects Chef Yoshida's Edo-mae training under a Tokyo master.
Located at 342 Bowery — the entrance is understated; look for the small placard street-level.
signatureDish: Seasonal 20-course Edomae omakase — the kohada (gizzard shad) cured in-house is a recurring standout

name: Adda

walkIn.advice: A few counter/bar seats are held for walk-ins; arrive right at 5 PM opening for the best shot.

tips: Unapologetic Foods group (Dhamaka, Semma, Adda) — books fast and rewards persistence.
Moved from Long Island City to the East Village (107 1st Ave); confirm the address.
Set an alarm for 8:55 AM — SevenRooms releases 7 days out at 9 AM and prime slots vanish.
Order the Butter Chicken Experience; the goat is the sleeper hit.

name: Ramen by Ra
tips: Drops twice a month — 1st and 15th at 9 AM ET. Set both alarms.
Counter omakase-style; you pick your bowl at booking. No walk-ins for the counter.
Relocated to a new East Village space in late 2025 — confirm the address on Resy.
Miss the drop? Takeaway broth cups and breakfast bao at the walk-up window until sold out.

name: Or'esh

walkIn.advice: Limited walk-ins — line up outside by 4 PM at the latest for the best shot.

tips: The hardest table in NYC in early 2026 — books out within seconds.
Reservations on SevenRooms (the DoorDash dining app) 7 days out at 10 AM ET.
Smart-elegant dress code enforced.
Michelin-starred chef Nadav Greenberg; go for the coal-fired seafood.
